phase1.explanation:
PrintWriter provides convenient text output:

PrintWriter features:
• println() — prints with newline
• print() — prints without newline
• printf() — formatted output (like C's printf)
• Autoflush option

PrintWriter vs PrintStream:
• System.out is a PrintStream
• PrintWriter handles character encoding better
• Use PrintWriter for Unicode text

Format specifiers:
• %d — decimal integer
• %f — floating point
• %.2f — float with 2 decimals
• %s — string
• %n — newline (platform independent)
• %x — hexadecimal
• %o — octal
• %e — scientific notation
• %t — date/time

Console input:
• BufferedReader + InputStreamReader + System.in
• System.console() (alternative, not always available)

Aligning output:
• %-10s — left-justified, width 10
• %10s — right-justified, width 10

PrintWriter vs System.out:
• PrintWriter can write to ANY file
• System.out only writes to console

Best practice: Use PrintWriter for file output, System.out for console.

  symbol:   variable pw
  location: class Test
1 error

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Creates a PrintWriter for a file called "grades.txt"
2. Writes a header: "Name	Grade"
3. Writes 3 student records in a table format
4. Uses printf for formatting (left-justified, width 10 for names)
5. Reads the file back and prints it

Bonus: Use try-with-resources for both operations.
dialogueHints: Use printf("%-10s %s%n", name, grade) for formatting
